title: Urban renewal in high-density urban areas from the perspective of crowd psychological health
summary: Accelerated urbanization has led to significantly increased urban density, amplifying associated challenges including strains on mental well-being. Concurrently, the saturation of traditional building stock emphasizes the critical importance of urban renewal initiatives. Current research in this domain often adopts singular perspectives: examples include analyzing pedestrian visual relationships using 3D-Isovist techniques, quantifying psychological perceptions through sensor-based data collection, or focusing on optimizing urban greenery configurations. While valuable, these approaches typically examine factors in isolation. A holistic framework integrating multiple dimensions is essential

title: Assessment of Photovoltaic Potential in Abandoned Industrial Mining Areas Based on a Double Machine Learning Framework: A Case Study of Xiaoyi City
summary: Within the ongoing energy transition of Chinese-style modernization, abandoned industrial mining areas are promising sites for photovoltaic deployment (PV), but require reliable assessment of solar resources and capacity. This study develops a prediction framework for large abandoned mining sites, evaluating ground and rooftop PV potential. Using Xiaoyi City, Shanxi Province, China, we mapped regional PV potential. A three-dimensional modeling and parcel-morphology clustering workflow using Grasshopper and ArcGIS Pro produced 54 samples across four parcel types.
